name: JLL Prism
description: JLL's digital operating environment for bringing property, workplace, service, and performance information into one client-facing view. It helps teams move from scattered updates to a more unified operational picture.
whyUseIt: Property teams need one place to connect service delivery, reporting, and client visibility. Prism-style platforms help organize building data, work activity, experience metrics, and performance views so owners and occupiers can see what is happening without waiting for manual status-chasing.
aiCapabilities: AI can help summarize trends, surface anomalies, and draft concise client-ready commentary from large volumes of service and operational data.
workflowStage: Portfolio visibility, service oversight, and client reporting
propertyManagerWorkflow: A property manager uses Prism to pull together service outcomes, KPI movement, and key asset updates before client meetings or monthly reporting cycles.
managerExpects: A clean, current picture of asset activity that can be used to answer owner questions quickly and spot issues before they become surprises.
aiWorkflowImpact: Instead of hand-building every summary from scratch, teams can use AI-assisted summaries to shorten reporting time and focus more attention on judgment and recommendations.
demoDescription: Review a dashboard for occupancy, service tickets, and energy trends, then turn the highlights into three talking points for an owner meeting.

vocabulary.definition: A dashboard or reporting surface designed for owner or occupier visibility rather than only internal operations.
A metric or event pattern that points to a change in building performance or service quality.
A measure tied to occupant or tenant perception, such as service response or comfort feedback.

beginnerMistakes: Treating the dashboard as truth without checking the source data
Loading too many widgets instead of highlighting what matters to the owner
Using the platform as a display tool only instead of a decision-support tool
